prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG gaslamp-industrial atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Blades in the Dark / Doskvol inspired industrial-gothic world map, diverse archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of a vast black Void Sea filled with many haunted industrial island groups, microcontinents, and smuggler routes.
Subject: diverse archipelago layout with uneven island distribution across a broad dark ocean: several large walled city-islands, microcontinents, tiny haunted islets, curved island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, fragmented industrial coasts, leviathan-hunter ports, smuggler islands, prison islands, ghost-field ruins, refinery islands, deathlands islands, fog marsh islands, coal hill islands, tundra islands, dark forest islands, mountain islands, and electroplasmic lightning tower islands.
Style/medium: Doskvol city district map expanded to archipelago scale, gothic industrial zones, criminal faction territories marked with non-text sigils, gaslamp fantasy atlas, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, no single dominant mainland, island groups sweep in organic arcs and clusters, full archipelago visible with black ocean border.
Lighting/mood: industrial gothic, perpetual supernatural darkness, smog and fog, flickering gas lamps, electroplasmic blue lightning, smuggling routes, criminal factions, hungry ghosts across the Deathlands.
Color palette: charcoal black, tarnished brass, cold blue electroplasm, sickly green fog, soot grey, muted brick red, oil-black ocean.
Materials/textures: blackened parchment, etched coastlines, gaslight glow, soot stains, canal-line geometry, detailed waves and shoals, ghostly blue technical overlays.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid grid-like spacing and avoid identical island shapes.
